evals.prompt: найди все открытые баги назначенные на меня в проекте BACKEND и покажи ключ, название и приоритет
evals.expected_output: Uses `atl -F toon j search "project = BACKEND" --status Open --type Bug --assignee currentUser() --fields 'key,summary,priority'` or equivalent. Project must go in JQL (no --project flag exists). Uses -F toon since Claude reads the output. Filter flags for status/type/assignee.

assertions.text: Uses -F toon (not -F json) since output is for Claude's context
Project is in JQL string, not a --project flag (which doesn't exist)
Uses filter flags (--status, --type, --assignee) for non-project filters
Specifies --fields to limit returned data
Uses currentUser() for --assignee, not a hardcoded account ID

evals.prompt: создай страницу в Confluence в пространстве TEAM с заголовком 'Sprint 42 Retro Notes', контент возьми из файла ~/docs/retro.md, положи её под родительскую страницу 98765
evals.expected_output: Uses `atl c create --space TEAM --title 'Sprint 42 Retro Notes' --body @~/docs/retro.md --input-format markdown --parent 98765`. Must use --input-format markdown (not --body-format). Must use @file syntax for body.

assertions.text: Uses --input-format markdown (not --body-format)
Uses @~/docs/retro.md syntax to read body from file
Includes --parent 98765
Uses --space TEAM (not --space-id)

evals.prompt: переведи задачу ORB-456 в статус Done
evals.expected_output: First runs `atl j transitions ORB-456` to get available transition IDs, then uses `atl j move ORB-456 --transition <ID>`. Must NOT use status name 'Done' directly — must look up the numeric transition ID first.

assertions.text: Runs atl j transitions ORB-456 first to get IDs
Uses numeric transition ID in atl j move (not status name 'Done')
Uses -F toon for the transitions lookup

evals.prompt: мне нужно обновить содержимое страницы 54321 в Confluence — замени body на файл updated-design.md, заголовок оставь прежним
evals.expected_output: First runs `atl -F toon c info 54321` to get current title and version number, then runs `atl c update 54321 --title '<current title>' --body @updated-design.md --version <N+1> --input-format markdown`. Must get version first, pass all three required flags.

assertions.text: Runs atl c info first to get current version and title
Uses info (not read) — lighter, no body fetched
Passes all three required flags: --title, --body, --version
Increments version by 1 from info result
Uses --input-format markdown

evals.prompt: покажи задачи в текущем активном спринте на доске 42, хочу видеть ключ, статус и assignee
evals.expected_output: First runs `atl -F toon j sprint list 42 --state active` to find the active sprint ID, then runs `atl -F toon j sprint issues <SPRINT_ID> --fields 'key,status,assignee'`. Board ID is positional (not --board-id flag).

assertions.text: Uses sprint list with board_id as positional arg (not --board-id)
Uses --state active to filter for active sprint
Follows up with sprint issues using the sprint ID from step 1
Specifies --fields to limit returned data

evals.prompt: напиши bash-скрипт который берёт все задачи в статусе 'To Do' из проекта PLATFORM и добавляет к каждой комментарий 'Reviewed in weekly sync'
evals.expected_output: Script uses -F json (not toon, because it's for scripting), --jq to extract keys, --all for complete results, and loops through keys calling atl j comment. Sets ATL_API_TOKEN or assumes it's set.

assertions.text: Uses -F json (not toon) since output is parsed by script
Uses --jq '.issues[].key' to extract issue keys
Uses --all to get complete results
Loops through keys and calls atl j comment for each

evals.prompt: загрузи файл diagram.png как вложение к странице 11111 в Confluence и покажи список всех вложений после загрузки
evals.expected_output: Runs `atl c attachment upload 11111 --file ./diagram.png` then `atl -F toon c attachment list 11111`.

assertions.text: Uses atl c attachment upload with page_id as positional arg
Uses --file flag for the file path
Follows up with attachment list to verify

evals.prompt: какие задачи были обновлены за последнюю неделю в проекте INFRA? отсортируй по дате обновления, сначала свежие
evals.expected_output: Uses `atl -F toon j search "project = INFRA" --updated-after 2026-04-06 --order-by updated --reverse` (date is today minus 7 days). Project goes in JQL, date/order use filter flags.

assertions.text: Project is in JQL string, not a --project flag
Uses --updated-after with a date approximately 7 days ago
Uses --order-by updated
Uses --reverse for descending order

evals.prompt: нужно получить список всех кастомных полей в Jira и вывести в CSV формате

assertions.text: Uses -F csv for CSV output
Uses --custom flag to filter for custom fields only
Does NOT use -F json with jq conversion — CSV is native

evals.prompt: создай задачу типа Bug в проекте CORE с высоким приоритетом, назначь на меня. Заголовок: 'Login page returns 500 on empty password'. В описании напиши шаги воспроизведения. Добавь лейблы auth и critical
evals.expected_output: Uses atl j create with all appropriate flags. Must use --assignee with a way to get current user's account ID (either atl j me first, or note that --assignee doesn't accept currentUser()). Must use --labels 'auth,critical' as comma-separated.

assertions.text: Uses --project CORE
Uses --issue-type Bug
Uses --priority High
Uses --labels with comma-separated values
Either gets account ID via atl j me or explains the limitation
Uses --description with reproduction steps

evals.prompt: нужно через atl api сделать POST запрос на endpoint rest/api/2/issue чтобы создать задачу с кастомным JSON payload из файла payload.json
evals.expected_output: Uses `atl api --service jira -X POST rest/api/2/issue --input @payload.json`. Must use --input (not --field/--raw-field) for file-based body.

assertions.text: Uses atl api --service jira
Uses -X POST for the HTTP method
Uses --input @payload.json (not --field or --raw-field)
Does NOT try to use atl j create for this (user explicitly asked for atl api)

evals.prompt: найди страницу с названием 'API Design Guidelines' в пространстве ENGINEERING, прочитай её содержимое и скажи мне основные разделы
evals.expected_output: First uses `atl -F toon c find --title 'API Design Guidelines' --space ENGINEERING` to get page ID, then `atl -F toon c read <PAGE_ID>` to read content. Uses -F toon for both since Claude reads the output.

assertions.text: Uses atl c find to locate the page by title first
Uses --space ENGINEERING to narrow the search
Then reads the page using the ID from find results
Uses -F toon for both commands
